book.title: The Aesop for Children
book.source: Aesop, ill. Milo Winter (Rand McNally, 1919). Public domain in the USA. Text via Project Gutenberg #19994.

level.title: The Crow and the Pitcher
level.moral: In a pinch a good use of our wits may help us out.

segments.value: , when the Birds could find very little to drink, a
Crow found a pitcher with a little water in it. But the pitcher was high and had a
, and no matter how he tried, the Crow could not reach the water. The poor thing felt as if he must die of

.

Then an idea came to him. Picking up some small pebbles, he
them into the pitcher one by one. With each pebble the water
until at last it was near enough so he could drink.
